summary: Counts a filtered advanced-search result set into broad award-type buckets. Live probing confirmed the award-level response shape and default spending_level=awards; the documented subaward variant remains unverified in this bundle.
businessPurpose: Use this endpoint to quickly answer how many filtered results fall into each major award category before drilling into records or dollar totals. It is a classification-count view over the advanced search filter system, not a spending-total endpoint.
analyticalGrain: One aggregate response object per request for the entire filtered population, broken into broad type buckets such as contracts, grants, loans, direct payments, other, and IDVs; a documented subaward variant may instead return subgrants and subcontracts.

primaryEntities.name: Filtered award population
primaryEntities.description: The set of prime awards matched by the advanced-search filters when spending_level is awards or subawards is false/default.

primaryEntities.name: Filtered subaward population
primaryEntities.description: The set of subawards matched by the advanced-search filters when spending_level is subawards or subawards is true, subject to the documented subaward-specific time-period and DEFC semantics.

primaryEntities.name: Award type bucket
primaryEntities.description: A high-level category such as contracts, grants, loans, direct payments, other, or IDVs that the endpoint uses as output buckets.

measures.name: Award count by bucket
measures.description: Numeric counts of filtered awards in each documented award-type bucket: grants, loans, contracts, direct_payments, other, and idvs.

measures.name: Subaward count by bucket
measures.description: A documented but not yet observed response variant that may count subgrants and subcontracts when grouping at the subaward level.

dimensions.name: Award versus subaward level
dimensions.description: The request can target award-level or subaward-level counting via spending_level and subawards.

dimensions.name: Advanced search filter set
dimensions.description: Counts can be sliced by the same advanced search dimensions documented for filters, including time period, agencies, recipient filters, award type filters, location filters, program filters, and disaster codes.

dimensions.name: Response type bucket
dimensions.description: The response itself is dimensioned by broad categorical buckets rather than by individual award records or geographic rows.

suitableQuestions.name: How many filtered results are contracts versus grants versus loans?
suitableQuestions.description: Use this endpoint when you need category counts for an advanced-search slice before deciding which detailed endpoint to query next.

suitableQuestions.name: Does my filter set mostly return IDVs or non-IDV awards?
suitableQuestions.description: The response includes an IDV bucket alongside the other broad award categories.

suitableQuestions.name: At a high level, how many subawards match these filters?
suitableQuestions.description: The documented subaward mode suggests a compact count view of subgrants versus subcontracts when subaward grouping is requested.

notSuitableFor.name: Dollar totals or obligations/outlays analysis
notSuitableFor.description: This endpoint is about counts by type, not spending amounts. Use a spending-total or award-detail endpoint for dollars.

notSuitableFor.name: Record-level award listings
notSuitableFor.description: The response is one aggregate object, not a paginated list of awards or subawards.

notSuitableFor.name: Fine-grained subtype or code-level distribution
notSuitableFor.description: The endpoint rolls filtered results into broad output buckets; it does not expose counts by every award_type_code in the response.

notSuitableFor.name: Pre-2008 historical search coverage
notSuitableFor.description: A live warning says search time periods are currently limited to an earliest date of 2007-10-01, so older-period analysis may need download endpoints.

joins.description: Use this endpoint as a preflight classification step, then send the same advanced-search filters to detail or amount-oriented search endpoints when you need records or dollars instead of counts.

on: Switch workflows when the needed time period predates 2007-10-01
joins.description: The live warning explicitly recommends custom award download or download endpoints for data going back to 2000-10-01.

workflows.name: Preflight award-type mix for an advanced search
workflows.description: Check the category mix of a planned advanced-search slice before selecting a more detailed downstream endpoint.

steps.action: Build the advanced-search filters object for the population you care about.
Call this endpoint to get broad count buckets for the filtered population.
If you need record details or dollar totals, reuse the same filters with a more detailed search endpoint rather than treating this count response as the final analytical output.

workflows.name: Historical-data fallback
workflows.description: Switch to download-style workflows when your requested period predates the search endpoint's current lower date limit.

steps.action: Attempt the search workflow only for periods at or after 2007-10-01.
Use custom award download or other download endpoints for data going back to 2000-10-01 when older history is required.

caveats.statement: The subaward response shape is only implied by documentation in this bundle; live behavior has not yet confirmed whether results switches to subgrants and subcontracts exactly as documented.
caveats.impact: Treat subaward-mode response parsing as provisional until observed live.

caveats.statement: DEFC filters have documented special rules that differ between prime-award and subaward counting.
caveats.impact: Counts may shift for reasons beyond simple population size when COVID-19 or IIJA disaster codes are present.

caveats.statement: Quoted award_ids are documented to behave as exact matches while unquoted award_ids use fuzzier matching.
caveats.impact: Seemingly small request-format differences can materially change the resulting counts.

caveats.statement: Live responses can warn that search dates are currently limited to an earliest date of 2007-10-01.
caveats.impact: For older historical analysis, plan a download workflow instead of assuming this endpoint covers the full 2000-forward period.
